info.name: Content Engine Admin API
info.description:
Complete admin API for the Content Engine backend.

Set these variables before running:
- baseUrl: http://localhost:3001
- adminKey: dev-admin-key-change-me

All admin routes require X-Admin-Key header (auto-injected via collection variable).
Public learner routes (/v1/topics, /v1/navigation) do not require auth.

request.description: Returns { status: 'ok', timestamp }. No auth required. Use to verify backend is running.
Returns total counts: languages, sections, topics, published_topics, total_blocks, quiz_questions, diagrams.
Returns per-language breakdown: total topics, published, unpublished, deep_dives, sections_used.
Returns block type distribution: how many text, concept_cards, code, diagram, quiz etc blocks exist.

item.name: 🚀  Bulk Insert
item.description: Insert complete topic or full language in one call

item.name: Bulk Topic
item.description: Insert or replace a full topic with all blocks

request.description:
Inserts a complete Java DSA Arrays topic.

Includes all block types:
- text
- concept_cards (6 cards)
- diagram (mermaid inline)
- code (runnable playground)
- note
- warning
- quiz x3

Quiz questions are automatically extracted into quiz_questions table.
Mermaid diagram stored in diagrams table.
Redis cache flushed automatically.

Inserts a deep dive child topic under java.dsa.arrays.

The path java.dsa.arrays.multidimensional automatically makes this a child of java.dsa.arrays.
isDeepDive: true flags it for the deep dive section.

Send AFTER the parent topic is created.
Minimal topic with text, concept_cards, and one quiz. Good template for new topics.

item.name: Bulk Language
item.description: Insert full language with sections and topics

request.description:
Inserts a complete Python language with:
- Language metadata (slug, name, color, tagline)
- 2 sections (core-language, advanced)
- 1 topic (python.basics) with blocks

Response includes ok, language, sections[], topics[], errors[]
HTTP 207 if any topics fail (partial success).
Create just the language and sections without any topics yet. Topics can be added later via /bulk/topic.
Returns all languages including inactive ones. Includes id, slug, name, iconUrl, meta, isActive, sortOrder.

Returns language details + all its sections.
Set langSlug variable to the language slug.
Create a new language. slug must be unique and URL-safe (lowercase, hyphens ok).
Update language fields. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated (COALESCE).

Sets is_active = false. Language is hidden from learners but data is preserved.
NOT a hard delete.
Returns all sections for a language, ordered by sort_order.
Create a new section under a language. slug must be unique within the language.
Update section fields. Set sectionId variable to the section UUID.
Hard delete a section. Topics that referenced it will have section_id set to NULL.

Paginated list of topics. Response includes total, limit, offset, topics[].
Filter by language, section, or search term.

Returns full topic including all blocks array.
Set topicPath variable e.g. java.dsa.arrays

item.name: Create & Update
item.description: Create new topics or update existing

request.description: Create a new topic. Uses { meta: {}, blocks: [] } envelope shape (same as PUT).

FULLY replaces topic metadata + all blocks.
All existing blocks are deleted and replaced with the new array.
To keep blocks, fetch the topic first and include existing blocks.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Arrays in DSA",
  "difficulty": "intermediate",
  "estimatedMins": 18,
  "isPublished": true,
  "tags": [
    "java",
    "dsa",
    "arrays"
  ]
}

request.description:
Updates only the metadata fields provided. Blocks are NOT touched.
Good for changing difficulty, publish status, sort order, tags.
All fields are optional.

Sets is_published = false. Topic is hidden from learners.
Data is preserved. Re-publish with PATCH { isPublished: true }.
Returns all diagrams. Optionally filter by type or search by key.
Returns diagram with full data content (svg string, mermaid source, or url).

Create a diagram with raw SVG/HTML content.
The svg field holds the full HTML string — typically generated by running npm run export:diagrams.

body.raw:
{
  "diagramKey": "java_dsa_bst",
  "title": "Binary Search Tree",
  "type": "mermaid",
  "data": {
    "source": "graph TD
  R[40] --> L[20]
  R --> RR[60]
  L --> LL[10]
  L --> LR[30]
  RR --> RL[50]
  RR --> RRR[70]
  style R fill:#1e3a5f,color:#58a6ff
  style L fill:#1a2e1a,color:#3fb950
  style RR fill:#1a2e1a,color:#3fb950"
  }
}

request.description: Create a diagram using Mermaid source. Frontend renders it client-side with the Mermaid library.
Update diagram content. Only provided fields are changed.
Hard deletes the diagram. Topic blocks referencing this diagramKey will show a placeholder until a new diagram with the same key is created.
Paginated list of quiz questions. Response includes total, limit, offset, questions[].
Returns full question with options array and explanation.
Create a standalone quiz question. Usually you embed questions in blocks via /bulk/topic instead.
Create a true/false question. questionType must be 'true-false'.
Update question fields. Only provided fields are changed.
Hard deletes the question. Topic blocks referencing this questionId will show a placeholder.

item.name: ⚡  Cache
item.description: Flush Redis cache after content changes

request.description:
Flushes ALL cached data — topics, nav trees, language list.
Use after running npm run seed or making bulk changes.
Returns count of deleted keys per category.

Flushes one topic response + its language nav tree.
Use after editing a specific topic.
Set topicPath variable first.

Flushes all topic responses. Nav trees are kept.
Good after a batch topic update.

Flushes the sidebar navigation tree for one language.
Use after adding, reordering, or renaming sections/topics.

Lists matching Redis keys. Use for debugging.
pattern supports * glob.
Examples:
  ce:topic:v1:java* — all Java topic cache entries
  ce:nav:v1:*       — all nav trees
  ce:*              — everything

item.name: 👁️  Public Learner API
item.description: Read-only routes used by the learner frontend (no auth required)

request.description: Returns active languages for the homepage language selector.

Returns full sidebar tree: language metadata + sections + nested topics.
Cached 1 hour. Flush with /admin/cache/flush { scope: 'nav' }.

Returns full topic: metadata + breadcrumb + children + enriched blocks.
Blocks include quiz question data and diagram content injected by the backend.
Cached 30 minutes.
Returns direct children only — used by sidebar lazy-loading.
Returns all descendants at any depth — used for section pre-fetching.
Returns ancestor chain root → leaf for the given path.

Returns a single quiz question normalised for the frontend Quiz component.
Response: { question: { q, opts, correct, exp } }
